channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The White House Updates Its AI Directives for Quicker Modernization - Federal Agencies Set to Adopt AI
item.description: The White House has released updated 'America First' AI directives, aimed at modernizing the federal government and adopting artificial intelligence in various capacities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Redbox goes up for auction following a wild bankruptcy saga
item.description: Redbox is getting ready for one final sale. The defunct DVD rental chain’s assets, and those of its corporate siblings Crackle and Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, are being auctioned off in New York later this month, according to a court filing published Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration
item.description: Max Everett, a former Energy Department CIO, offers some advice for how new agency technology leaders can deal with some basic challenges. The post Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby losing popularity – Tiobe index
item.description: The Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby languages have slipped from their top 20 positions in the Tiobe index of programming language popularity. All three seem to have lost traction and to be in decline, according to Tiobe CEO Paul Jansen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI commentary goes into full swing at Players Championship
item.description: As a golf fan, one of the events I look forward to is the Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass at the home of the PGA Tour in Ponte Vedra, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: World’s first AI-powered industrial super-humanoid robot
item.description: Mech the super-humanoid robot can lift up to 132 pounds and is designed to tackle stressful and repetitive tasks that often lead to workplace injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to free up space on your Android
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers some of his easy expert tips to free up storage space on your Android if your device is running short on storage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: My dog recognizes the sounds a Waymo car makes
item.description: Most of us know the general (albeit simplified) story: Russian physiologist Ivan Pavlov used a stimulus—like a metronome—around the dogs he was studying, and soon, the hounds would start to salivate. They had learned that the sound meant food was coming.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure
item.description: Researchers found the algorithms that rank pages in internet searches also can help planners understand how to prevent failures in electrical systems. The post How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Why Trump's tariffs are so bad for Big Tech
item.description: President Trump’s sweeping new slate of tariffs is poised to strike a blow at the tech industry, as massive import taxes on China and Taiwan disrupt trade flows that are central to tech firms’ business models.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Sonatype warns of 18,000 open source malware packages
item.description: Software supply chain security company Sonatype uncovered 17,954 open-source malware packages during Q1 2025, the company revealed in its Open Source Malware Index. Sonatype’s Open Source Malware Index for Q1 2025 was introduced April 2.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ServiceNow to acquire Logik.ai to boost CRM portfolio
item.description: ServiceNow is acquiring Logik.ai and its configure, price, quote (CPQ) solution to expand its customer relationship management (CRM) product portfolio and accelerate its momentum in sales and order management. Logik.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Relentless hackers abandon Windows to target your Apple ID
item.description: Experts say hackers who used to focus on Windows operating systems are increasingly targeting Apple IDs as part of a new phishing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: Coverage of Nvidia GTC 2025 With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: And it's coming with a load of new features ranging from 4K, 120 frames per second support, a new LCD screen.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation
